post.description:
Authenticates an existing email/password user and returns an ID token.
Use the returned `id_token` as the bearer token for endpoints protected by `bearer_auth`.
This endpoint is only relevant when the user signs in with an email/password credential that
already exists in Firebase Authentication.

Use the token for requests to this API.

Creates a authentication user record for the supplied external `user_id`.
This endpoint only provisions the user id. It does not set an email, password, or profile fields.
Use it when your integration manages user identities outside this API and wants to ensure the
account exists before issuing sign-in tokens.

Creates a custom token for a user.
The returned `token` is not itself the bearer token for this API. A client must exchange it
with authentication and then use the resulting ID token as `bearer_auth`.
When `create_if_not_exists` is `true` (default), the user record is created automatically if
it does not already exist. Optional `user_data` is stored alongside the external user record
and can be used to attach organization context or other integration metadata.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of material ids that are currently searchable for an organization.
The inventory is organization-specific and reflects the loan providers, access rules, and
filtering configured for that organization in WeDoBooks.
This endpoint only returns material ids (ISBN-13). Use the metadata or ONIX endpoints to fetch
descriptive data for the returned ids.

The authenticated API client must be explicitly allowed to access the target organization.
The organization can be resolved either by `organization_id` or `third_party_organization_id`.

parameters.description: A string to identify the organization in question. This id is either provided by WeDoBooks or the organization itself per agreement will all parties involved. The credentials of the caller needs to allowed to access the inventory on behalf of the organization.
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: Alternative string to identify the organization in question. This is intended to be the ISIL of the organization in question if it exists. If you want to use this id then this needs to be configured in our system, so please let us know.
parameters.name: third_party_organization_id

parameters.description: A string to identify the last retrieved item for pagination. Use this to continue pulling data from where the last request left off. The pagination_cursor will be updated and returned on each request until there is no more data to be fetched.
parameters.name: pagination_cursor

parameters.description:
The maximum number of items to retrieve. If the specified value exceeds 1000, it will be capped at 1000.
If omitted, the endpoint defaults to `1000`.
parameters.name: pagination_limit

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of loans.
By default only active loans are returned because `active` defaults to `true`. Set
`active=false` to retrieve ended or inactive loans instead.
Use `user_id` to limit the list to a single user. Pagination uses the returned loan id as the
next `cursor`.

parameters.description: Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` to page inactive or ended loans.
parameters.name: active

parameters.description: Optional user filter. Omit to page across users.
parameters.name: user_id

post.description:
Attempts to create a loan for a user and material immediately.
This endpoint performs the same availability and quota checks as `GET /v1/loans/can-loan`.
When a loan is created, the response status is `201` and the `loan` object is populated. When
the material cannot be loaned, the response is still a successful API response with a decision
status and optional rejection details, but without a `loan`.

get.description:
Checks whether a user can currently loan a specific material without creating a loan.
Use this as a preflight check when you want to show availability, provider choice, or quota
rejection details before calling `POST /v1/loans`.
The result reflects the state at request time only and should not be treated as a reservation
or guarantee that a later loan request will still succeed.

parameters.description: ISBN-13 of the material the user wants to loan.
parameters.name: material_id

parameters.description: User identifier.
parameters.name: user_id

get.description:
Returns a single loan by its `loan_id`.
Use this endpoint to refresh the authoritative state of a loan after creation or when syncing
an external system with loan status in WeDoBooks.

Returns metadata for up to 50 materials in a single request.
The input uses ISBN-13 values in `material_ids`. Materials that do not exist are silently
omitted from the response instead of failing the entire request.
Use this endpoint together with inventory or reservation/loan data when you need catalog
fields such as title, author, language, description, and publisher metadata.

parameters.description: Up to 50 ISBN-13 identifiers to resolve in a single request.
parameters.name: material_ids

get.description:
Returns metadata for a single material id.
Despite the singular path, the response shape is the same as the batch endpoint and still
returns a `materials` array containing zero or one item.
Use this when you already know the ISBN-13 and want the same metadata structure as the bulk
endpoint.

Returns a single material as ONIX 3.0 XML.
The exact output depends on the authenticated client's API context, so a material may exist in
WeDoBooks internally but still return `404` if it is not exposed in the caller's context.
Use this endpoint when you need the current ONIX representation for one ISBN-13 instead of a
paginated export feed.

parameters.description: ISBN 13
parameters.name: material_id

get.description:
Returns a page from the full ONIX export for the authenticated client's context.
`onix_products` contains an ONIX 3.0 XML document with up to `pagination_limit` products.
Start without a cursor and continue until the returned pagination cursor is empty. The final
page includes `next_delta_pull_utc_timestamp`, which should be stored and later used when
switching to delta pulls.

Access to alpha pulls is restricted per API client and returns `401` when the client is not
enabled for this export mode.

parameters.description: A string to identify the last retrieved item for pagination. Use this to continue pulling data from where the last request left off. The pagination_cursor will be updated and returned on each request until there is no more data to be fetched.
parameters.name: pagination_cursor

parameters.description:
The maximum number of items to retrieve. If the specified value exceeds 100, it will be capped at 100.
If omitted, the endpoint defaults to `100`.
parameters.name: pagination_limit

get.description:
Returns a page from the incremental ONIX export for the authenticated client's context.
The response contains an ONIX 3.0 XML document with products updated since
`from_utc_timestamp`. Continue paging with `pagination_cursor` until the cursor is empty.
The final page includes `next_delta_pull_utc_timestamp`, which should be stored as the start
point for the next delta synchronization.

`from_utc_timestamp` must be a UTC ISO 8601 timestamp with millisecond precision and may not
be older than one month.

parameters.description: onix metadata for books edited after this UTC date in ISO 8601 format
parameters.name: from_utc_timestamp

parameters.description: A string to identify the last retrieved item for pagination. Use this to continue pulling data from where the last request left off. The pagination_cursor will be updated and returned on each request until there is no more data to be fetched.
parameters.name: pagination_cursor

parameters.description:
The maximum number of items to retrieve. If the specified value exceeds 100, it will be capped at 100.
If omitted, the endpoint defaults to `100`.
parameters.name: pagination_limit

get.description:
Returns organization details resolved by either the internal `organization_id` or an external
`third_party_id`.
Use this endpoint to resolve an organization before making organization-scoped calls or when
you need display, hierarchy, contact, or branding data for the organization.
At least one identifier must be provided.

parameters.description: WDB internal id. 20 characters. `id` on the organization model
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: External id - use if you don't have the WDB internal id. For example a national municipality or institution id
parameters.name: third_party_id

get.description:
Returns the organization configuration for an organization resolved by
`organization_id` or `third_party_id`.
Use this endpoint when your integration needs organization-level settings that influence client
behavior. Internal library system details and card credentials are intentionally excluded from
the response DTO.

parameters.description: WDB internal id. 20 characters. `id` on the organization model
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: External id - use if you don't have the WDB internal id. For example a national municipality or institution id
parameters.name: third_party_id

post.description:
Requests a reservation for a user and material.

If the material can be loaned right away, the endpoint creates a loan instead of a waiting
reservation and responds with status `201`. Otherwise it returns status `200` with a
reservation-oriented decision payload.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of reservations.
Use `user_id` to limit the response to a single user, or omit it to page through reservations
across users. When `limit` is omitted, the default page size is `25`.
The returned pagination cursor is the reservation id to send back on the next request.

parameters.description: Optional user filter. Omit to page across users.
parameters.name: user_id

post.description:
Accepts an active reservation offer and converts it into a loan.
Either `offer_id` or `reservation_id` can be used.

A reservation offer temporarily holds the relevant license for the title and price model.
On expiry/cancellation the license will be released.
On success, the response includes the created `loan_id` and uses status `201`.

The reservation and the offer are cleaned up shortly after acceptance, so external systems
should not expect those records to remain available indefinitely.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of reservation offers for a single user.
`user_id` is required. Use this endpoint to discover offers before calling
`POST /v1/reservations/accept-offer`, or to inspect offer status and timestamps.
The returned pagination cursor is the reservation offer id to send back on the next request.

parameters.description: User whose reservation offers should be returned.
parameters.name: user_id

get.description:
Returns a single reservation by `reservation_id`.
Use this to refresh the authoritative state of a known reservation.
A previously accepted or deleted reservation may return `404` if it has already been removed.

delete.description:
Deletes an existing reservation.
Use this when the user no longer wants to wait for the material.
The response indicates whether the underlying reservation record was successfully removed.

get.description:
Returns the curated shelf layout for an organization and context.
Use `context` to choose between the organization's `education` and `library` curation setup.
The response is grouped into blocks by age range and contains the referenced collection,
standard, and theme shelves in display order.
If the organization has no curation configured for the requested context, the endpoint returns
an empty `blocks` array instead of an error.

parameters.description: WDB internal id. 20 characters. `id` on the organization model
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: External id - use if you don't have the WDB internal id. For example a national municipality or institution id
parameters.name: third_party_id

get.description:
Returns borrowing quota information for a user across every organization the user belongs to.
Use this endpoint when you need to show remaining loan capacity or organization-specific quota
rules before attempting a loan.

The response may contain multiple entries for the same user, one per organization. Each entry
contains the organization id and name, ancestor ids, whether quotas are split by format or
shared across formats, and only the relevant quota fields for that mode.

Treat this as informational state at request time. Loan creation endpoints remain
authoritative because availability and quota usage can change between calls.

When `split_on_format` is `true`, the response includes per-format loan limits and usage for
audiobooks and ebooks. When `split_on_format` is `false`, the response includes combined loan
limits and usage across formats.

Returns the support id for a user.
If the user does not already have a support id, one is generated and persisted before the
response is returned.

info.description:
## Rate limit

All API calls are subject to rate limiting per API consumer. The API will respond with status code 429 if an API consumer is a bit too enthusiastic with the frequency of calls so be prepared to back off if that's the case.

The rate limit is implemented using the "sliding window" method with a default max of 100 calls per 60 seconds. If you exceed this threshold 5 times you will be locked out and will have to reach out to us to have access restored.

The rate limit values _can_ be adjusted if absolutely necessary, so get in touch if you feel like it's necessary.

## Authentication

Use the [/auth/sign-in](#operation/SignInWithPassword) endpoint to get the short-lived token needed to call the rest of the endpoints. 
Place the API key in an \`x-api-key\` header and pass the email and password credentials as a JSON body.

The short lived token received from the [/auth/sign-in](#operation/SignInWithPassword) call should be place as a Bearer token in the \`Authorization\` header for calls to all other endpoints.

The API key needs to be present in all API calls.

## How to use the Onix endpoints

1. Alpha pull: First time you use the Onix API you must retrieve all the books metadata using the [alpha pull endpoint](#operation/GetAlpha). Alpha pull is not active by default. Please contact us in order to activate alpha pulls for your account.
2. Delta pull: Use the [delta pull endpoint](#operation/GetDelta) to retrieve updates (created/updated products).

Both alpha and delta pulls are paginated. You will be able to fetch up to 100 products per request.

Once the last page of an alpha or delta pull is reached, a \`next_delta_pull_utc_timestamp\` will be returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format with milliseconds precision. It must be stored in the client's system and be used for the next delta pull.

You can also get metadata for a specific book by ISBN using the [book onix metadata](#operation/GetOnix) endpoint


## Webhooks

Registered endpoints will receive HTTP POST requests when resources change. All webhook events share a common envelope format.

### Request

**Method:** `POST`
**Content-Type:** `application/json`

### Envelope

```json
{
  "type": "<event_type>",
  "action": "create" | "update" | "delete",
  "data": { ... }
}
```

| Field    | Type     | Description                                                                 |
|----------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `type`   | `string` | Identifies the resource that changed. See types below for possible values. Use this to route events on your end. |
| `action` | `string` | One of `create`, `update`, `delete`.                                        |
| `data`   | `object` | The resource payload. Shape depends on `type`.                              |

### Expected Response

Return any `2xx` status code to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses or network errors are logged on our side but do not trigger retries within the same invocation.

---

### Event Types

#### `reservation_offer`

Fired when a reservation offer is created, updated (e.g. status change), or deleted.

**`data` fields (ReservationOfferDto):**
See return type of [Get reservation offers](#operation/GetReservationOffers)

**Example:**

```json
{
  "type": "reservation_offer",
  "action": "create",
  "data": {
    "id": "0047BKfxp5vjXVNwGWyL",
    "reservation_id": "004HtOh1s2W7pnifFXZC",
    "uid": "004l4QzdSEb4ehKX1Smz",
    "material_id": "9788711623671",
    "material_type": "ebook",
    "org_id": "004olTeagCyw9FF1IEaa",
    "origin_org_id": "004olTeagCyw9FF1IEaa",
    "origin_org_type": "municipality",
    "provider": "package",
    "license_id": "005RvbmU7NrWSamdddbx",
    "created_at": "2026-04-01T10:00:00.000Z",
    "expires_at": "2026-04-10T23:59:59.000Z",
    "status": "offered"
  }
}
```
info.title: Biblio API

expires_in_seconds.description: Lifetime of the returned ID token in seconds.

email.description: Email address of a email/password user.

password.description: Password for the supplied email address.

success.description: `true` when the user record exists after the request.

token.description: Custom token. Exchange it for an ID token before calling bearer-auth endpoints.

expires_in_seconds.description: Lifetime of the custom token in seconds.

organization_third_party_id.description: Third party id for the municipality

tags.description:
0 or more tags. Only for `institution` type currently.
If more than one is supplied the first supported one will
be used. If none are supplied the default settings for the client type are used.
Tags can apply special loan quotas.
The current list of supported institution tags are: `BOERNETILBUD, AELDRETILBUD,
BIBLIOTEKET_KOMMER, ADGANG_4, ADGANG_5, ADGANG_6, ADGANG_7,
ADGANG_8, ADGANG_9, ADGANG_10`

create_if_not_exists.description: Create user record if one does not already exist

user_data.description: Optional external user payload persisted for later organization/user resolution.

limit.description: Number of items returned per page.

cursor.description: Identifier to use as the starting point for the next page.

material_ids.description: Material ids (ISBN-13) available in the organization's searchable inventory.

start.description: Loan start timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

end.description: Loan end or expiry timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

last_opened_at.description:
Last time the loan was opened, in ISO 8601 format. 
Only available if maintained by clients.

active.description: Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` to page inactive or ended loans.

user_id.description: Optional user filter. Omit to page across users.

status.description: Current loan decision returned by the loan manager.

loan_provider.description: Loan provider selected for the attempted loan, when available.

active_loan_provider.description: Indicates whether the selected provider is currently active for the user/org context.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the loan decision.

org_id.description: Organization that governed the decision, when relevant.

quota_reject.description: Structured quota rejection details when quota rules block the loan.

loan.description: Present only when a loan was actually created.

material_id.description: ISBN-13 of the material the user wants to loan.

status.description: Current loan decision returned by the loan manager.

loan_provider.description: Loan provider selected for the attempted loan, when available.

active_loan_provider.description: Indicates whether the selected provider is currently active for the user/org context.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the loan decision.

org_id.description: Organization that governed the decision, when relevant.

quota_reject.description: Structured quota rejection details when quota rules block the loan.

loan.description: Loan details for the requested id.

Pick_MaterialInformation.author-or-categ.description: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

materials.description: Metadata records for the requested ids. Unknown ids are omitted.

material_ids.description: Up to 50 ISBN-13 identifiers to resolve in a single request.

onix_products.description: ONIX 3.0 XML document containing the products for the current page.

next_delta_pull_utc_timestamp.description: Timestamp to persist and reuse as `from_utc_timestamp` for the next delta sync.

population.description: population of the organization's area (unrelated to user/loaner count) - used for calculating selections prices

is_strict.description:
- A strict organization will always be the primary
- A patron can only have one strict organization

Pick_OrganizationData.Exclude_keyofOrgan.description: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

population.description: population of the organization's area (unrelated to user/loaner count) - used for calculating selections prices

is_strict.description:
- A strict organization will always be the primary
- A patron can only have one strict organization

organization_id.description: WDB internal id. 20 characters. `id` on the organization model

third_party_id.description: External id - use if you don't have the WDB internal id. For example a national municipality or institution id

lower.description: `true` if library allows loan for lower educations.

higher.description: `true` if library allows loan for higher educations.

Pick_LibraryConfig.Exclude_keyofLibraryC.description: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

Omit_LibraryConfig.library_system-or-car.description: Construct a type with the properties of T except for those in type K.

CombinedLoanConfig.description: Loan configurations for all material types.

reservation_offer_duration_days.description: Only used for projects where user has to accept reservation offer
Only used for projects where user has to accept reservation offer

selected_order.description: Prioritized order of active loan providers.

split_on_format.description: If `true` organization uses {@link loan_config} else uses {@link combined_loan_config}.

Pick_OrganizationConfigs.Exclude_keyofOr.description: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

split_on_format.description: If `true` organization uses {@link loan_config} else uses {@link combined_loan_config}.

status.description: Current loan decision returned by the loan manager.

loan_provider.description: Loan provider selected for the attempted loan, when available.

active_loan_provider.description: Indicates whether the selected provider is currently active for the user/org context.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the loan decision.

org_id.description: Organization that governed the decision, when relevant.

quota_reject.description: Structured quota rejection details when quota rules block the loan.

loan.description: Present only when the reservation request was fulfilled immediately as a loan.

user_id.description: User that should receive the reservation or immediate loan decision.

success.description: `true` when the offer was accepted and converted into a loan.

loan_id.description: Loan id created from the accepted offer. Present only on success.

reservation_id.description: Reservation id whose active offer should be accepted.

material_type.description: Material type at reservation creation time.

org_id.description:
The organization that made the reservation possible.
Loan can be created on another organization if material will be available sooner

type.description: Either `Reservation` or `Wish`. `SystemReservation`: wish was converted to reservation.

offer_id.description: Active reservation offer id when this reservation has been offered to the user.

org_ids.description: User organizations (including ancestors) at reservation create time

word_count.description: Word count snapshot used for reservation prioritization and analytics.

loan_date.description:
Date where reservation is expected to be converted to loan at latest
based on material license and organization loan config.

offer_expires_at.description: Expiry time of the currently linked reservation offer.

user_id.description: Optional user filter. Omit to page across users.

material_id.description: Material made available by the offer.

material_type.description: Material type of the offered title.

org_id.description: Organization that provided the held availability.

uid.description: User that owns and may accept the offer.

reservation_id.description: Source reservation that produced this offer.

origin_org_id.description: User organization used when resolving the held provider allocation.

provider.description: Provider whose availability is being retained during the offer window.

license_id.description: Provider-specific identifier for the retained license/budget allocation.

status.description: Current lifecycle state for the offer.

loan_id.description: Final created loan id after acceptance.

user_id.description: User whose reservation offers should be returned.

organization_id.description: WDB internal id. 20 characters. `id` on the organization model

third_party_id.description: External id - use if you don't have the WDB internal id. For example a national municipality or institution id

split_on_format.description: Split quotas are enabled, so only per-format values are returned

max_user_loans_period.description:
Period used when evaluating `max_user_loans` per format.
The total duration can be at most 3 months.

max_concurrent_user_loans.description: Maximum concurrent loans allowed per format

current_monthly_loans.description: Current loans counted within the configured loan period per format

split_on_format.description: Split quotas are disabled, so only combined values are returned

combined_max_concurrent_user_loans.description: Maximum concurrent loans allowed across formats

combined_current_monthly_loans.description: Current loans counted within the configured loan period across formats

combined_max_user_loans_period.description:
Period used when evaluating `combined_max_user_loans`.
The total duration can be at most 3 months.

bearer_auth.description: Short lived bearer token obtained from auth endpoint
